actor_relation_claims.quote_text: the Executive Committee of the Penford board
Perkins Coie LLP (referred to as Perkins Coie), Penford’s legal counsel
Deutsche Bank as the Company’s financial advisor
Mr. Behrens, the SEACOR representative on the board
Mr. Behrens, the SEACOR representative on the board
An engagement letter with Deutsche Bank was executed on September 11, 2014.

event_claims.actor_label: Executive Committee of the Penford board
event_claims.description: Notice was provided of an Executive Committee meeting to discuss Schedule 13D filings and strategic developments, including discussions with Ingredion.
event_claims.quote_text: on July 22, 2014, Mr. Lawlor, as secretary of the Company, provided notice of a meeting of the Executive Committee of the Penford board for the purpose of discussing, among other things, recent Schedule 13D filings, including the SEACOR Filing and strategic developments, including the discussions with Ingredion.

event_claims.description: The Executive Committee held a telephonic meeting with all members present, along with other directors, management and Perkins Coie representatives.
event_claims.quote_text: On July 24, 2014, the Executive Committee of the Penford board held a telephonic meeting, at which all members of the Executive Committee, as well as certain other directors, members of Penford’s management and representatives of Perkins Coie LLP (referred to as Perkins Coie), Penford’s legal counsel, were present.

event_claims.description: The Executive Committee directed management to retain Deutsche Bank as financial advisor and to advise on shareholder matters and strategic alternatives.
event_claims.quote_text: Following discussion of potential financial advisors, the Executive Committee directed management to proceed to retain Deutsche Bank as the Company’s financial advisor and to advise on shareholder matters and to evaluate strategic alternatives, and directed management to negotiate a suitable engagement letter.

event_claims.description: The board discussed the Executive Committee’s decision to engage Deutsche Bank.
event_claims.quote_text: The board discussed the new credit facility, a recent meeting of the Executive Committee and the Executive Committee’s decision to engage Deutsche Bank with regard to shareholder matters and to evaluate strategic alternatives.

event_claims.description: The Executive Committee met with management, Perkins Coie and Deutsche Bank to discuss Ingredion's August 10 indication of interest.
event_claims.quote_text: On August 11, 2014, the Executive Committee met with members of management and representatives of each of Perkins Coie and Deutsche Bank, at which meeting they discussed Ingredion’s indication of interest received on August 10.

event_claims.description: The Executive Committee determined that further review and investigation of Ingredion's indication of interest was in the best interests of the Company and shareholders.
event_claims.quote_text: Following further discussion among the Executive Committee members, the Executive Committee unanimously determined that it would be in the best interests of the Company and its shareholders to proceed with further review and investigation of Ingredion’s indication of interest to acquire Penford.

event_claims.description: The Executive Committee determined that Mr. Hatfield should contact Mr. Behrens to discuss whether SEACOR had interest in acquiring the Company.
event_claims.quote_text: the Executive Committee determined that Mr. Hatfield should promptly contact Mr. Behrens, the SEACOR representative on the board, to discuss whether SEACOR had an interest in acquiring the Company.

event_claims.description: The Executive Committee indicated Mr. Behrens should recuse himself from future board sale-process discussions if SEACOR had acquisition interest due to a potential conflict.
event_claims.quote_text: The Executive Committee indicated that if SEACOR did have such an interest, Mr. Behrens should recuse himself from future board discussions related to a possible sale process due to the potential conflict between Mr. Behrens’ duties to the Company’s shareholders and his duties to SEACOR.

event_claims.description: Mr. Behrens stated that SEACOR was not interested in participating in any potential sale process at that time.
event_claims.quote_text: On August 12, 2014, Mr. Hatfield discussed the Ingredion indication of interest with Mr. Behrens to determine if SEACOR was interested in acquiring the Company. Mr. Behrens stated that SEACOR was not interested in participating in any potential sale process at that time.

event_claims.description: Mr. Behrens confirmed SEACOR's position that it was not interested in participating in any potential sale process at that time before substantive board discussions.
event_claims.quote_text: Prior to engaging in substantive discussions, Mr. Hatfield requested that Mr. Behrens confirm the position of SEACOR with respect to a potential transaction with Penford. Mr. Behrens confirmed that SEACOR was not interested in participating in any potential sale process at that time.

event_claims.description: The board authorized management and Deutsche Bank to proceed with a market check process.
event_claims.quote_text: The board also authorized management and Deutsche Bank to proceed with the market check process as described at the meeting.

event_claims.description: Perkins Coie reviewed and led a discussion of the fiduciary duties of Penford's board in connection with change-of-control strategic proposals.
event_claims.quote_text: As part of the August 28, 2014 board meeting, Perkins Coie provided a review, and led a discussion, of the fiduciary duties of Penford’s board, including in connection with strategic proposals involving a change in control of Penford.
